title: Renormalization group analysis for bosonization coefficients in half-odd-integer Kitaev spin chains
authors: Jianxun Li, Chao Xu, Wang Yang
abstract: Based on a renormalization group (RG) analysis, we study the bosonization formulas in spin-S Kitaev-Gamma and Kitaev-Heisenberg-Gamma chains in the (K < 0, Gamma > 0, J > 0) parameter region, where S is a half-odd integer. We find that the effects associated with the breaking of emergent continuous symmetries in bosonization formulas scale as 1/S in the large-S limit, which is in qualitative agreement with DMRG numerical results for Kitaev-Gamma chains. In Kitaev-Heisenberg-Gamma chains, symmetry analysis reveals ten independent bosonization coefficients, five of which are predicted by the RG analysis to have no dependence on the Heisenberg coupling up to linear order. Our work may offer valuable input for determining magnetic ordering tendencies in two-dimensional Kitaev spin models within a quasi-one-dimensional approach.
summary: This paper provides an analytical framework for the bosonization of spin-S Kitaev-type chains using renormalization group theory. It demonstrates that the effects of broken emergent symmetries vanish in the large-S limit and provides specific predictions for the coefficients that govern magnetic ordering.
main_problem: Determining the bosonization coefficients for spin-S Kitaev-Gamma and Kitaev-Heisenberg-Gamma chains and understanding how the breaking of emergent continuous symmetries scales with spin S.
main_result: The study finds that symmetry-breaking effects in bosonization formulas scale as 1/S in the large-S limit and identifies ten independent bosonization coefficients in the Kitaev-Heisenberg-Gamma model, five of which are independent of Heisenberg coupling to linear order.
method: Renormalization group (RG) analysis combined with one-loop diagrammatic calculations and validation via Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) numerical simulations.
model_or_system: One-dimensional spin-S Kitaev-Gamma and Kitaev-Heisenberg-Gamma chains, which serve as quasi-1D models for 2D Kitaev materials like alpha-RuCl3.
key_observables: Bosonization coefficients (C and D), spin correlation functions, and scaling of symmetry-breaking terms.
important_parameters: Spin quantum number S (half-odd-integer), Kitaev coupling (K), Gamma interaction (Gamma), Heisenberg exchange (J), and the parameter delta-Gamma.
main_assumptions: Small delta-Gamma limit, large-S limit for scaling relations, and neglecting higher-order terms in delta-Gamma and J.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the ratio of bosonization coefficients as a function of parameter phi; Figure 3 and 4 show diagrammatic representations of the RG vertex and Feynman diagrams for scaling field renormalization; Figure 5 shows log-log plots of spin correlation components.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the spin-S Kitaev models, performs a microscopic derivation using a fermionic representation, applies RG analysis to calculate the renormalization of scaling fields, validates results with DMRG, and discusses the large-S scaling behavior.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2: Bond structures (a) before and (b) after the six- sublattice rotation. The rectangular boxes denote unit cells.

FIG. 1: C1/C2 as a function of ϕ for S = 3/2 (blue curve) and S = 1/2 (orange curve) in Kitaev-Gamma chains, where K = cos(ϕ), Γ = sin(ϕ). DMRG numerics are performed on systems of L = 144 sites under periodic boundary conditions. Bond dimension m and truncation error ϵ in DMRG numerics are taken as m = 3000, ϵ = 10−7.
FIG. 3: Vertex of (a) interaction and (b) scaling field.
FIG. 4: Diagrams for the renormalizations of scaling fields.

FIG. 5: (a) sx i (j) as a function of rL on a log-log scale (i = 0, 1, 2) and (b) sx(r)rL as a function of r obtained from DMRG numerics at ϕ = 0.8π for S = 3/2 Kitaev-Gamma chain, where K = cos(ϕ), Γ = sin(ϕ). DMRG numerics are performed on systems of L = 144 sites under periodic boundary conditions. Bond dimension m and truncation error ϵ in DMRG numerics are taken as m = 3000, ϵ = 10−7.
